_meta.note: Daemon-backed lookup for one live probe by probe_id. Unknown probes are typed errors; success returns the same bounded row shape used by probe_list. v1.1 adds TC11 frames_suppressed* counters (additive).

unknown_probe_error_example.message: daemon ipc error [UnknownProbe]: probe 'prb_018f0000000000000000000000009999' is not live in any runtime

invariants: probe_id is required and uses the probe id wire prefix
probe_status input is probe_id, not job_id or watch_id
success payload wraps one probe row under probe
success probe row uses the same bounded shape as probe_list
success payload never contains argv, bytes_total, cursor, rules, cwd, env, stdout, stderr, screen buffers, file content, or event bodies
unknown probes return a typed UnknownProbe error, not null, empty success, or synthetic inactive state
